**What:** Volume high/intensity low at start of block, inverse trend across weeks toward a peak.
**Who:** Beginners-intermediates; single-goal blocks (e.g., pure strength peak).
**When to use:** Contest prep, off-season base building, clients needing simple-to-follow progression logic.
**When not:** Multi-goal concurrent training, elite athletes needing simultaneous quality maintenance across qualities.
**AI Rule:** `IF training_age < 12 months THEN default to linear periodization within each mesocycle (simpler autoregulation burden).`
**What:** Rep ranges/intensity vary session-to-session or week-to-week rather than block-to-block.
**Who:** Intermediate-advanced lifters, hypertrophy+strength concurrent goals.
**When to use:** 3+ sessions/week for same pattern, plateau-breaking, variety-driven adherence.
**When not:** True beginners (too much simultaneous variable management), fatigue-management-priority clients (orange/yellow tier).
**AI Rule:** `IF confidence_tier == "green" AND training_age >= 12 months AND frequency_per_pattern >= 2 THEN prefer daily undulating over linear.`
**What:** Sequential blocks each targeting one quality (accumulation → intensification → realization/peak).
**Who:** Advanced lifters, competitive powerlifters/Olympic lifters.
**When to use:** Pre-competition cycles, advanced trainees with well-established base.
**When not:** General fitness clients, beginners, clients without a competition/peaking date driving the plan.
**AI Rule:** `IF client.goals.primary in ["powerlifting","olympic_lifting"] AND client.goals.target_date is not null AND training_age >= 24 months THEN offer block periodization option.`
**What:** Rotating exercise variation on "max-effort" day while maintaining volume on "dynamic/repetition effort" days.
**Who:** Advanced lifters seeking to manage staleness on primary lifts without abandoning specificity entirely.

**AI Rule:** `IF client.training_age >= 36 months AND client.goals.primary == "powerlifting" AND client reports staleness/plateau on main lift THEN offer max-effort-variant rotation for that lift only, not full conjugate system overhaul.`

**Caution:** True conjugate (Westside-style) programming requires dense coaching oversight; the AI should offer a simplified variant-rotation version, not a full autoregulated conjugate system, unless coach override (file 14 Section 2.3) explicitly requests it.
**What:** Using bar-speed thresholds to autoregulate load/set termination instead of fixed %1RM.
**Who:** Advanced lifters with velocity-tracking equipment access.
**AI Rule:** `IF client.equipment.available contains "velocity_tracker" AND training_age >= 24 months THEN offer VBT autoregulation option, else default to RPE/RIR-based autoregulation (file 2).`
**Velocity Loss Cutoffs (general guidance):** Stop set at 20-25% velocity loss for hypertrophy-biased work, 10-15% for strength/power-biased work to preserve bar speed qualities.
